title: Overview of the format and content for post-market drug benefit-risk assessment in Canada – guidance document - Canada.ca
content:
## Overview of the format and content for post-market drug benefit-risk assessment in Canada – guidance document
- Amendments to the Food and Drugs Act: Guide to New Authorities
- Food and Drug Regulations
The objective of this guidance document is to assist Market Authorization Holders (MAHs) in developing a post-market benefit-risk assessment for a marketed drug when requested.

## Who this guide is for
- Market Authorization Holders (MAH) for marketed drugs in Canada
- Consultants who prepare post market benefit-risk assessments
- 1 Introduction 1.1 Objective 1.2 Scope 1.3 Background 1.3.1 Benefit-Risk Assessment as Part of the Health Product Lifecycle 1.3.2 Purpose of a Benefit-Risk Assessment 1.3.3 Regulatory Background
- 1.3 Background 1.3.1 Benefit-Risk Assessment as Part of the Health Product Lifecycle 1.3.2 Purpose of a Benefit-Risk Assessment 1.3.3 Regulatory Background
- 1.3.1 Benefit-Risk Assessment as Part of the Health Product Lifecycle

- 1.3.3 Regulatory Background
- 2 Preparing a Post-Market Benefit-Risk Assessment 2.1 Background Information 2.1.1 Purpose 2.1.2 Product Information 2.1.3 Regulatory History 2.1.4 Exposure Estimates 2.1.5 Information on the Disease(s) or Condition(s) 2.1.6 Other Therapeutic Options 2.2 Evaluation of Benefit 2.2.1 Characterization of Benefit(s) 2.2.2 Discussion of Comparative Benefits 2.3 Evaluation of Risk 2.3.1 Characterization of the Identified Risk 2.3.2 Considerations of the Issue (Identified Risk) 2.3.3 Discussion of Comparative Risks 2.4 Evaluation of the Benefit-Risk Profile 2.4.1 Summary and Analysis of the Benefit-Risk Profile for the Identified Risk 2.4.2 Quantitative Benefit-Risk Analysis 2.4.3 Benefit-Risk Analysis in Context 2.5 Conclusions and Risk Mitigation Options 2.5.1 Conclusions 2.5.2 Risk Mitigation Options
